About Me
Driven by Code,
Defined by Purpose

Hi, I am Rosshed Joseph L. Bibon, a dedicated student pursuing a Bachelor in Computer Engineering Technology at Batangas State University Lipa, currently enrolled in section BCPET 2201.

Passionate about bridging hardware and software, I am committed to developing deep technical skills in computer engineering while cultivating creativity in design and problem-solving. I believe technology has the power to transform lives, and I aspire to be part of that transformation.

Senior High School Thesis — April 2023
AI Alcohol Sensing & Car Engine Locking System
Holy Family Academy — STEM Strand
Arduino C++ MQ-3 Sensor IoT AI STEM Research
Assistive Prototype AI Alcohol Sensing & Car Engine Locking System
A capstone research project developed at Holy Family Academy for the prevention of Driving Under the Influence (DUI) cases in Padre Garcia, Batangas. The system uses an MQ-3 alcohol sensor and an Arduino Uno R3 Atmega328 microcontroller to detect blood alcohol concentration via breath proximity. When alcohol is detected, a buzzer and red LED activate and the hobby gearmotor (simulating the car engine) automatically locks. The prototype was tested at three BAC levels (0.03%, 0.05%, 0.08%) across three distances (25cm, 40cm, 60cm), proving most effective at 25cm. Co-authored with John Cedrick Liangco, Claire Macasaet, John Ivan Magmanlac, Mariel Pacuin, and Katrina Arabella Sales. Advised by Mr. Harold Carpio and Ms. Arriane J. Conti.
Read Full Paper View Results
